content:
5 min read 9 hours ago -- Press enter or click to view image in full size I watched a developer with eight years of Angular experience spend an entire afternoon debugging a screen that simply refused to update. No error. No warning. Just a value that changed in the debugger and never once changed on the screen. He knew OnPush. He knew signals. He knew every optimization trick in the book. And none of that saved him, because the bug was not about knowing the rules. It was about a trap that only shows up once you already trust the rules enough to stop questioning them. Three traps. All three have humbled developers far more experienced than either of us. Read carefully, because the danger here is not ignorance, it is confidence. Trap 1: Mutating Data Instead of Replacing It, Under OnPush The problem OnPush is one of the first optimizations every serious Angular developer reaches for. It also has one condition that is easy to forget the moment you stop consciously thinking about it, it only checks for a new reference, not new content. @Component({ selector: 'todo-list', changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: `
{{ t.text }}
` }) export class TodoListComponent…
